claim: relativizeScanError() can still expose workspace absolute paths in data.errors when path-like tokens contain non-excluded delimiters such as colon or NUL.
counterevidenceSought: Long all-matching input completed in linear time; newline-separated paths were individually relativized; whitespace/bracket paths did not retain the workspace root; the existing regression test covers a single embedded path but not delimiter-joined variants.
alternativeExplanation: Colon can be a valid POSIX filename character and NUL cannot be a real filesystem path character, but the helper redacts parser/persistence error strings where multiple absolute paths may be joined before serialization.
downgradeTrigger: Downgrade if all producers of scan errors prove they never include joined absolute path strings, or if the tokenizer/redactor is changed to redact every absolute-path segment independently.
